title: LVMH FY2025 Annual Results
content: PARIS — LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton, the world's leading luxury group, today published its results for fiscal year 2025. Revenue was €85.7 billion, down 2% as reported but with organic growth of 3% in H2 2025, confirming a sequential improvement in luxury demand after a challenging H1. Fashion and Leather Goods segment revenue was €42.2 billion (-3% organic), led by Louis Vuitton and Christian Dior; both Maisons continued to outperform peers. Selective Retailing (Sephora, Le Bon Marché, DFS) contributed €18.0 billion (+5% organic), driven by Sephora's expansion and beauty resilience. Watches and Jewellery generated €10.8 billion (-4% organic) reflecting the macro headwinds in aspirational luxury. Wines and Spirits revenue was €6.6 billion (-8% organic) due to Cognac inventory normalisation by distributors. Perfumes and Cosmetics reached €8.1 billion (+2% organic). Operating profit was €19.6 billion with an operating margin of 22.9%. Net profit attributable to LVMH shareholders was €7.2 billion. The board proposed a total dividend of €13.00 per share for 2025. Bernard Arnault, Chairman and CEO: 'LVMH is uniquely positioned for the long term. Our portfolio of 75 exceptional Maisons, combined with our vertical integration and heritage craftsmanship, provides durable competitive advantages that no rival can replicate quickly.' LVMH has 6,196 stores globally and employs 213,000 people. The company is the largest holding in the CAC 40 index by market capitalisation.
